title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 11 (Fragment 4)
text:
“Let’s get the Hangman’s rope,” said Angelica.

They edged along to the pile of puppets and disentangled the string from the little gibbet. Tonino tied it to the table leg.

“It’s a long way down,” he said dubiously.

“It’s only a few feet really ,” Angelica said. “And we’re not heavy enough to hurt. I went all floppy when you kicked me off the stage and—well—I didn’t break anything anyway.”

Tonino let Angelica go first, swinging down into the dark space like an energetic blue monkey. Crunch went the shoddy table. Creeeak . And it swayed towards the leg where the rope was tied.

“Angel of Caprona!” Tonino whispered.

The table plunged, one corner first, down into the space. The cardboard room rattled. And, with a rending and creaking of wood, the table stuck, mostly in the hole, but with one corner out and wedged against the sides. There was a thump from below. Tonino was fairly sure he was stuck in the room for good now.

“I’m down,” Angelica whispered up. “You can pull the rope up. It nearly reaches the floor.”

Tonino leaned over and fumbled the string up from the table leg. He was sure there had been a miracle. That leg ought to have broken off, or the table ought to have gone down the hole. He whispered “Angel of Caprona!” again as he slid down under the table into the dark.

The table creaked hideously, but it held together. The string burned Tonino’s hands as he slid, and then it was suddenly not there. His feet hit the floor almost at once. “Oof!” he went. His feet felt as if they had been knocked up through his legs.

Down there, they were standing on the shiny floor of a Palace room. The towering walls of the Punch and Judy show were on three sides of them. Instead of a back wall, there was a curtain, intended to hide the puppet-master, and very dim light was coming in around its edges. They pulled one end of the curtain aside. It felt coarse and heavy, like a sack. Behind it was the wall of the room. The puppet show had evidently been simply pushed away to one side. There was just space for Angelica and Tonino to squeeze past the ends of the show, into a large room lit by moonlight falling in strong silver blocks across its shiny floor.

It was the same room where the court had watched the Punch and Judy show. The puppet show had not been put away. Tonino thought of the time he and Angelica had tottered on the edge of the stage, looking into nothingness. They could have been killed. That seemed another miracle. Then, they must have been in some kind of storeroom. But, when the Duchess was so mysteriously taken ill, no one had bothered to put them back there.

The moonlight glittered on the polished face of the Angel, high up on the other side of the room, leaning out over some big double doors. There were other doors, but Tonino and Angelica set out, without hesitation, towards the Angel. Both of them took it for a guide.

“Oh bother!” said Angelica, before they reached the first block of moonlight. “We’re still small. I thought we’d be the right size as soon as we got out, didn’t you?”

vocabulary.definition: rough or harsh in texture or manner
vocabulary.example: It felt coarse and heavy, like a sack.

vocabulary.definition: to make a long high sound when something moves or is moved
vocabulary.example: And, with a rending and creaking of wood, the table stuck, mostly in the hole, but with one corner out and wedged against the sides.

vocabulary.definition: to free something from an entangled or twisted state
vocabulary.example: They edged along to the pile of puppets and disentangled the string from the little gibbet.

vocabulary.definition: in a doubtful or uncertain manner
vocabulary.example: “It’s a long way down,” he said dubiously.

vocabulary.definition: showing or involving great activity or vitality
vocabulary.example:
”

Tonino let Angelica go first, swinging down into the dark space like an energetic blue monkey.

vocabulary.definition: limp or soft and hanging loosely
vocabulary.example: I went all floppy when you kicked me off the stage and—well—I didn’t break anything anyway.

vocabulary.definition: to do something awkwardly, especially with the hands
vocabulary.example:
”

Tonino leaned over and fumbled the string up from the table leg.

vocabulary.definition: to shine with a bright, shimmering reflected light
vocabulary.example: The moonlight glittered on the polished face of the Angel, high up on the other side of the room, leaning out over some big double doors.

vocabulary.definition: serving to show the way; acting as a guide
vocabulary.example: Both of them took it for a guide.

vocabulary.definition: the action of pausing before doing something
vocabulary.example: There were other doors, but Tonino and Angelica set out, without hesitation, towards the Angel.

vocabulary.definition: in a very ugly or shocking way
vocabulary.example: The table creaked hideously, but it held together.

vocabulary.definition: to be hit or jostled upwards suddenly
vocabulary.example: “I’m down,” Angelica whispered up.

vocabulary.definition: an extraordinary event that cannot be explained by natural laws
vocabulary.example: He was sure there had been a miracle.

vocabulary.definition: to fall or leap suddenly into something
vocabulary.example: The table plunged, one corner first, down into the space.

vocabulary.definition: made smooth and glossy usually by rubbing
vocabulary.example: The moonlight glittered on the polished face of the Angel, high up on the other side of the room, leaning out over some big double doors.

vocabulary.definition: to make a rapid succession of short, sharp knocking sounds
vocabulary.example: The cardboard room rattled.

vocabulary.definition: to tear something into pieces violently
vocabulary.example: And, with a rending and creaking of wood, the table stuck, mostly in the hole, but with one corner out and wedged against the sides.

vocabulary.definition: of poor quality or badly made
vocabulary.example: Crunch went the shoddy table.

vocabulary.definition: to move through a narrow space by applying pressure
vocabulary.example: There was just space for Angelica and Tonino to squeeze past the ends of the show, into a large room lit by moonlight falling in strong silver blocks across its shiny floor.

vocabulary.definition: a room where things are stored
vocabulary.example: Then, they must have been in some kind of storeroom.

vocabulary.definition: extremely tall, especially in comparison to others
vocabulary.example: The towering walls of the Punch and Judy show were on three sides of them.

vocabulary.definition: to force something tightly into a narrow space
vocabulary.example: And, with a rending and creaking of wood, the table stuck, mostly in the hole, but with one corner out and wedged against the sides.
